_comment: Smoke fixture: Rule 3b — irreconcilable evidence override. Action-vote is 2:1 (escalated, escalated, resolved) which would normally be Rule 2. But the incomplete worker identifies a concrete observable (partner bucket ACL ownership) that is dispositive AND not retrievable with available tools — meeting all three criteria for the irreconcilable-evidence override per investigate-merge POST.md §Rule 3. Expected: Rule 3 fires. escalate-to-stage-c called with action_class=ambiguous, flags=[system-genuinely-uncertain]. All 3 evidence chains compiled in reason. (Action-divergence path 3a is impossible by construction — deep workers only emit resolved|escalated, so 3-way action divergence cannot occur with only 2 actions; Rule 3 in practice is always the 3b override.)

finding.evidence: Large BigQuery export (8.2GB) to external GCS bucket gs://partner-analytics-export-acme. Service account is in baseline. Export volume is 4x the 90-day mean.

partial_transcript_summary: Parent investigate: called check-baseline (SA known, 312 export events, mean export 2.1GB), search-events (single export event, no companion events), search-findings (1 related finding 21 days ago, resolved benign — same SA, similar volume). Confidence 0.49 — fell below 0.55 threshold. Fan-out triggered.

verdict.reason: Found positive confirming evidence of legitimacy. read-config shows partner-analytics-export-acme is a registered partner bucket in the data-sharing configuration with scheduled quarterly exports. This event date matches Q1 quarterly export window. Volume spike (8.2GB vs 2.1GB mean) is consistent with quarterly aggregation.

evidence_chain: read-config: data-sharing config lists gs://partner-analytics-export-acme as approved partner bucket with scheduled Q1/Q2/Q3/Q4 exports
Event date: 2026-01-15 — Q1 quarterly export window (Jan 10-20)
baseline-stats: service account shows quarterly volume spikes in historical data — consistent with pattern
No privilege change: SA had existing export permissions, no new grants
Benign confirmed: documented workflow, expected partner destination, consistent timing

verdict.reason: Volume anomaly (4x mean) to external bucket triggers exfiltration pattern. Prior benign resolution 21 days ago does not clear this incident. External bucket ownership requires independent verification — partner bucket could have been hijacked or misconfigured to accept external writes. Service account compromise could mimic scheduled export timing.

evidence_chain: Volume: 8.2GB is 4x the 90-day mean (2.1GB) — anomalous even accounting for quarterly spikes
search-findings: prior finding 21 days ago resolved benign — but prior resolutions do not clear new incidents
Credential theft test: compromised SA would time exfiltration to match expected schedule — not distinguishable
External bucket gs://partner-analytics-export-acme: read-config shows approved, but bucket could have been redirected (GCS bucket name squatting or partner account compromise)
Missing: verification that partner bucket ownership unchanged since last export

verdict.reason: Two critical data points are missing and irresolvable from available tools: (1) GCS bucket ACL to verify current ownership of partner bucket — bucket squatting risk is real. (2) Whether the export contents match the data-sharing agreement scope. Without these, the quarterly-export benign hypothesis and the exfiltration malicious hypothesis cannot be distinguished with confidence.

evidence_chain: Observable 1: partner bucket ACL (gs://partner-analytics-export-acme) — if owned by expected partner, benign; if ownership changed, malicious
Data retrieval: GCS IAM API — needs connector with storage.buckets.getIamPolicy permission; current connector lacks this
Observable 2: exported data schema/content — if matches partner agreement scope, benign; if includes out-of-scope tables (PII, financials), escalate immediately
read-config: current GCP connector does not capture export content schema — gap in coverage
Conclusion: two unresolvable gaps. Escalate for analyst to verify bucket ownership and export scope via GCP console.

expected_aggregation._note: While malicious+incomplete both say escalated, their evidence chains are irreconcilably different: malicious suspects active compromise, incomplete says data is missing for any verdict. The benign worker has a plausible evidence chain that directly contradicts both. This is a genuine 3-way irreconcilable divergence in the evidence picture.
